briefdescription.para: Special value to mark that the display width was not yet calculated.
The base code point followed by up to two combining code points.
The color for this character.
The cached display width of this character.
Construct an empty block character using inherited colors.
Construct a block character from a single Unicode code point using inherited colors.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char32_t codePoint, const Color color) noexcept
memberdef.name: Char

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character from a single Unicode code point and a color.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char32_t codePoint, const Foreground fgColor, const Background bgColor) noexcept
memberdef.name: Char

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character from a single Unicode code point and explicit colors.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char32_t codePoint, const Foreground::Hue fgColor, const Background::Hue bgColor) noexcept
memberdef.name: Char

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char32_t codePoint, const Foreground fgColor) noexcept

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Construct a block character with inherited colors.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-8 encoded text to display.
If the text is not valid UTF-8, contains U+0000, or uses more than three code points.

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character with inherited colors.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-32 encoded text to display.
If the text contains U+0000 or uses more than three code points.

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character with inherited colors.

parameterdescription.para: The zero-terminated UTF-32 encoded text to display.
If the text contains U+0000 or uses more than three code points.

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character with explicit text and colors.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-8 encoded text to display.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Construct a block character with explicit text and colors.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-8 encoded text to display.
If the text is not valid UTF-8, contains U+0000, or uses more than three code points.

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character with explicit text and colors.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-32 encoded text to display.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Construct a block character with explicit text and colors.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-32 encoded text to display.
If the text contains U+0000 or uses more than three code points.

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character with explicit text and colors.

parameterdescription.para: The zero-terminated UTF-32 encoded text to display.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Construct a block character with explicit text and colors.

parameterdescription.para: The zero-terminated UTF-32 encoded text to display.
If the text contains U+0000 or uses more than three code points.

briefdescription.para: Compare two terminal characters for equality.
Compare two terminal characters for inequality.
Get the character string as UTF-8 text.

simplesect.para: A UTF-8 encoded copy of the stored character sequence.

briefdescription.para: Get the leading Unicode code point.

simplesect.para: The base code point, or 0 if this character is empty.

briefdescription.para: Get the stored Unicode code points.
Get the number of stored Unicode code points.
Get the character color.
Get the display width on a terminal in cells.
Get the number of UTF-8 bytes needed to encode this character.
Append the UTF-8 representation to an existing buffer.
Create a character with an additional combining code point appended.

parameterdescription.para: The combining code point to append.

simplesect.para: A copy of this character with the combining code point appended.

parameterdescription.para: If codePoint is a control code, is not zero-width, or if this character already stores three code points.

briefdescription.para: Create a character with the given colors applied as an overlay.

simplesect.para: A copy of this character with the adjusted colors. If the applied colors contain Inherited, the existing component from this character is kept unchanged.

briefdescription.para: Create a character with the given color replacing the stored color.

simplesect.para: A copy of this character with exactly color.

briefdescription.para: Create a character with a base color underneath the stored color.

simplesect.para: A copy of this character where this character color overlays color.

briefdescription.para: Test if this character is spacing.

detaileddescription.para: Tests for space, tab, newline, and CR.

briefdescription.para: Compare how two characters would appear on screen.

detaileddescription.para:
Code points must match exactly. When colorEnabled is true, inherited color components are treated as the terminal default color before comparing.
other
The character to compare with.
colorEnabled
true to include colors in the comparison.
true if both characters render identically.

briefdescription.para: Represents a character string with foreground and background colors.

detaileddescription.para: Used by the UI code to render colored text blocks on the console.
